This classification places it within the group of mammillate cacti, characterized by their flowers and fruit-bearing structures. The genus Rapicactus is a monotypic genus, consisting of only one species, Rapicactus zaragosae. It is native to Mexico and is often found in mountainous regions. This classification helps to understand the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of Rapicactus zaragosae in the plant kingdom.
